title: First Customer Finder
verdict: The strongest early demand comes from owners manually chasing failed membership payments and asking peers how to handle them.

prospects.name: Riverside Fitness (example)
prospects.pain_signal: The owner publicly described manually following up on overdue memberships and asked how other owners handle the same workflow.
prospects.evidence: A recent public forum thread shows the owner describing the manual reminder cadence, the time cost, and asking for alternatives.
prospects.why_fit: The product automates the exact reminder and payment step the owner is doing by hand.
prospects.why_now: The thread is recent and the owner is actively asking for a better approach.
prospects.source_title: How do you handle failed payments? (example)
prospects.opener: Saw your question about failed-payment follow-up. The manual chase is exactly what we automated for a couple of small gyms. Mind if I share the one-line version?
prospects.caution: Confirm the workflow is still active before pitching.

prospects.name: Corner Gym (example)
prospects.pain_signal: The owner posted about spending hours each month reconciling failed card retries against a spreadsheet.
prospects.evidence: A public post describes the spreadsheet workaround and the monthly time cost without naming a tool.
prospects.why_fit: The product replaces the spreadsheet reconciliation step with automatic retry and reporting.
prospects.why_now: The post is a few months old, so freshness is lower, but the workaround is ongoing.
prospects.opener: Your spreadsheet-reconciliation process sounded familiar. We replaced that exact step for two gyms. Worth a quick look?
prospects.caution: Verify the owner is still at the same gym before outreach.

prospects.name: Northgate Fitness (example)
prospects.pain_signal: The gym publicly announced a billing-system migration and asked members for patience during the transition.
prospects.evidence: A public announcement describes an in-progress billing migration and asks for patience, implying a current gap.
prospects.why_fit: The product can run alongside the migration as a fallback for failed payments during the cutover.
prospects.why_now: The migration is in progress, so the timing trigger is live but the buyer is busy.
prospects.suggested_channel: Public contact form or reply on the announcement
prospects.opener: Noticed your billing migration note. During cutovers, failed retries usually spike. We built a fallback that catches them. Useful as a safety net for the next few weeks?
prospects.caution: Approach as a safety net, not a replacement, during their migration.

patterns.title: Manual follow-up
patterns.insight: Owners rely on spreadsheets and messaging apps after payments fail.

patterns.title: Cutover vulnerability
patterns.insight: Billing migrations create a temporary window where failed retries go unhandled.

outreach_plan.angle: Offer a manual concierge test before asking for software adoption.
outreach_plan.first_step: Contact the three highest-scoring prospects with one source-based question.
outreach_plan.follow_up: Share a two-minute mockup only after they confirm the pain.
outreach_plan.success: Three conversations and one design-partner commitment within seven days.

limits: These are potential customers inferred from public signals, not confirmed buyers.
Verify current relevance before any outreach and keep contact manual.
Source URLs are example placeholders in this fixture.
